emails.a:
Subject: 189 City Road — £19,411 net return over 25 years, on your own roof

The solar potential at 189 City Road points to a 25-year NPV of £19,411 against a capital outlay of £169,421 — a 12-year payback on an asset Lloyds Banking Group already owns.

The system generates £14,044 in annual electricity savings from day one, insulating that cost centre from wholesale price movements without leasing additional infrastructure or changing your energy contracts.

This is grid independence financed by your existing roof, not a speculative outlay.
emails.b:
Subject: Your next electricity contract renewal — and the roof that changes it

UK commercial electricity prices have risen 78% since 2019. Every contract renewal is another negotiation you enter from a weaker position, with no structural hedge on the grid costs sitting inside your P&L at 189 City Road.

A rooftop solar installation converts that exposure into a fixed, depreciating asset. The generation is yours. The £14,044 annual saving does not move with wholesale markets, supplier margins, or policy levies.

Lloyds Banking Group has the roof. The question is whether the next renewal is the last one you face on the grid's terms.
emails.cta: Would a 20-minute call this week be useful to walk through the site-specific figures?

title.headline: Grid Independence for 189 City Road
title.subhead: A fixed-cost generation asset on a roof Lloyds Banking Group already owns — insulating operating expenditure from GB wholesale market volatility.
title.decision_maker: Prepared for Lloyds Banking Group Plc

problem.heading: The Grid Is Not a Stable Cost Line
bullets: UK commercial electricity prices have risen approximately 78% since 2019, with no structural reason for that trajectory to reverse.
GB wholesale market exposure means every budget cycle carries an energy cost range, not a figure — a direct drag on forecasting confidence.
Imported electricity carries a marginal cost that compounds annually; a roof asset sitting idle produces nothing and offsets nothing.
Regulatory and carbon reporting obligations are tightening; unabated grid consumption increasingly requires disclosure and justification.

solution.heading: Turn the Roof at EC1Y 8AF Into a Generating Asset
bullets: 101 panels across the available roof area at 189 City Road deliver 32,310 kWh of generation annually at a fixed marginal cost of zero per unit.
Every unit self-consumed directly displaces a unit purchased from the grid at prevailing — and rising — commercial rates.
Surplus generation is exported under the Smart Export Guarantee, creating a secondary revenue stream from capacity that would otherwise be wasted.
A single capital decision today locks in a known cost structure for 25 years, converting an unpredictable operating expense into a depreciating balance-sheet asset.

grid_independence.heading: Sovereignty Over Your Energy Cost
grid_independence.body: At 32,310 kWh annual generation, this system offsets a material share of 189 City Road's imported electricity. Each percentage point of self-sufficiency is a percentage point permanently removed from GB wholesale market exposure — a structural improvement to the cost base, not a one-cycle saving.

roi.heading: Financial Case — Verbatim from Site Assessment

funding.heading: Five Routes to the Same Asset — Choose the Structure That Fits

models.name: Capital Expense
models.fit: Strongest NPV; Lloyds claims SEG and full capital allowances from year 1.

models.name: Free Install (PPA)
models.fit: Zero capex; suits constrained budgets, though provider retains SEG and ownership.

models.name: Lease Purchase
models.fit: Spreads capex over ~7 years; balloon payment transfers ownership at term end.

models.name: Operational Lease
models.fit: Off-balance-sheet rental; payments fully tax-deductible; SEG retained by client.

models.name: Hire Purchase
models.fit: Asset finance over ~5 years; title and capital allowances transfer from year 1.

decision_maker_callout.heading: A Note to the Decision-Maker at Lloyds Banking Group
decision_maker_callout.body: This proposal is structured for scrutiny at CFO level. The payback period of 12.06 years sits within the asset's 25-year warranted life, generating a positive NPV of £19,411 on a £169,422 outlay. Annual cash savings of £14,045 are defensible against rising grid tariffs. Capital allowances are available from year 1 under an outright purchase, improving the after-tax position materially. The numbers here are drawn directly from the site assessment — no rounding, no optimistic assumptions.

cta.heading: Commission the Full Survey
cta.body: The next step is a no-obligation structural and electrical survey of 189 City Road to confirm roof loading, DNO connection capacity, and the optimal panel configuration. This survey locks in the figures presented here against actual site conditions and initiates the G99 application clock. There is no cost to proceed to survey stage.
